"'''Resurrection'''" is a 48-page comic story that appeared in ''[[Star Wars Tales 9]]'' and collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 3]]''. The story was written by [[Ron Marz]], with pencils by [[Rick Leonardi]], inks by [[Terry Austin]], letters by [[Steve Dutro]] and colors by [[Raúl Treviño]]. It takes place shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]].

==Plot summary==
[[File:DVvDM_Resurrection.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Darth Maul confronts Darth Vader.]]
[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] arrives on the volcanic moon [[Kalakar Six]] in order to intercept [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] passing on stolen plans for the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. Sensing something familiar after landing, Vader dispatches his squad of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to scout the area. However, the stormtroopers are quickly cut down by a mysterious assassin, wielding a red [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber.]]

Vader enters a hollowed out volcano and is confronted by a number of [[Prophets of the Dark Side]]. They inform him that the information about the Rebels is false, and that they brought him here because they don't think he is fit to serve the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] as they claim he is "Not the blood of [[Darth Bane/Legends|Bane]]". As he attempts to strike the prophets down, he is blocked by [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]]. The Prophets resurrected Maul to replace Vader at the Emperor's side, and they goad Vader into dueling with the former Sith apprentice.

The pair launch into a furious duel, hurling insults at each other. They battle through the volcano onto a bridge situated over a river of lava. The bridge collapses, but both [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] narrowly escape falling in and the duel continues.

Vader manages to split his opponent's saber in two, but both sides remain intact. Maul strikes Vader on the helmet, causing him to fall to the floor. As Maul moves in for the killing blow, Vader uses [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to retrieve his lightsaber and stabs it through his own stomach, killing the enemy behind him. Maul, before he dies, questions what Vader could hate enough to destroy him. Vader's response: "Myself."

As Vader confronts the Prophets, they are electrocuted by [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]. He has arrived to check on Vader's progress, though he denies any involvement with the Prophets. He informs Vader there is much to do in stopping the Rebels with the stolen plans, and Vader dutifully follows his master to a waiting shuttle.

==Release and reception==
There are references to the Prophets of the Dark Side being on [[Dromund Kalakar]] in part 5 of the [[The Dark Forces Saga|''Dark Forces Saga'']].

''Resurrection'' was selected as one of [[Star Wars Insider 83|''Star Wars Insider'' 83]]{{'s}} ''[[20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe]]''.
